What students consistently liked: Beautiful green campus, excellent libraries, plentiful study spaces, strong academic resources, attractive environment. Frequently described as one of Exeter's biggest strengths.
Common criticisms: A few comments about inefficient facilities management or subject-specific facilities being weaker than expected.

What students consistently liked: Lecturers are generally knowledgeable, approachable, enthusiastic and supportive. Many students praised staff expertise and engagement.
Common criticisms: Some inconsistency between lecturers; a few complaints about lecturers reading from slides, communication difficulties, or varying teaching quality.

What students consistently liked: Broad curriculum, interesting modules, opportunities to specialise later in the degree, good academic depth.
Common criticisms: Some students wanted more elective choices or found certain core modules poorly structured.

What students consistently liked: Many students felt lecturers were accessible and willing to help. Study-skills support was appreciated.
Common criticisms: The single most repeated academic complaint was limited contact time. Several students felt they received too few taught hours each week.

What students consistently liked: Active societies, strong community feel, vibrant student life, lots of events and activities. Often highlighted as a major reason students enjoy Exeter.
Common criticisms: Some criticism of the Students' Guild/Union being bureaucratic or less impactful than expected.

What students consistently liked: A few affordable campus amenities were mentioned positively.
Common criticisms: One of the strongest negative themes. Sports clubs, gym memberships, accommodation, food and some social activities were repeatedly described as expensive.

What students consistently liked: Some students praised wellbeing and support services.
Common criticisms: A recurring minority concern was social culture. Several comments mentioned Exeter feeling less inclusive for working-class students, concerns about diversity, and one comment specifically referenced LGBTQ+ inclusivity.

What students consistently liked: Attractive city, safe environment, lots of green space, good campus atmosphere.
Common criticisms: Repeated complaints about steep hills, distance from cheaper accommodation, and parts of campus feeling far from town.
